title: GAUL 2024 Administrative Boundaries — Africa
description: FAO Global Administrative Unit Layers (GAUL) 2024 edition for Africa, providing polygon boundaries at admin levels 0 (country), 1 (province/state), and 2 (district/county). Distributed as GeoParquet files per administrative level. Covers all 54 African Union member states.

cdh:temporal_resolution.description: Annual release; boundaries represent administrative divisions as of 2024

cdh:variable_summary.name: admin0
cdh:variable_summary.interpretation: National administrative boundaries for 54 African Union member states. Each feature includes ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 and alpha-3 country codes, GAUL country code, and official country name. Suitable for continental-scale analysis and national-level reporting.

cdh:variable_summary.name: admin1
cdh:variable_summary.interpretation: First-level administrative subdivisions (provinces, states, regions, governorates depending on country). Each feature includes the admin1 name, GAUL admin1 code, and parent country identifier. Typical use: sub-national food security reporting and climate indicator aggregation.

cdh:variable_summary.name: admin2
cdh:variable_summary.interpretation: Second-level administrative subdivisions (districts, counties, departments, woredas depending on country). Each feature includes the admin2 name, GAUL admin2 code, and parent admin1 identifier. Finest spatial unit in this release; suitable for localised agricultural monitoring and early warning systems.

cdh:usecases: Spatial aggregation of gridded climate and agroclimatic indicators by administrative unit for national reporting
Food security early warning — linking FEWS NET alerts and IPC classifications to subnational boundaries
Crop production and yield statistics by admin zone for agricultural planning
Zonal statistics for climate adaptation programme targeting at district level
Boundary harmonisation for multi-country datasets spanning the African continent
